findings.description: Title and header styling differ from the source PDF.
findings.evidence: On page 1, the PDF uses a compact centered table title in uppercase with bracketed subtitle beneath, while the HTML renders a larger left-aligned heading with a horizontal rule and more spacious spacing. Information is preserved, but visual distinction and alignment are changed.

findings.description: The HTML includes fabricated wrapper/template content not present in the PDF, including standalone HTML metadata and imported stylesheet references.
findings.evidence: HTML contains text/metadata such as "<!DOCTYPE html>", "<html lang=\"en\">", "<title>Converted Document</title>", CSS comments "Digital Commons NC — Standalone Theme CSS", and "@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com...')". These do not appear anywhere in the source PDF pages, which only show the table and notes.

findings.description: A table value is incorrect for fiscal year 2007-2008: the Department of Commerce transfer amount was transcribed as 800,000 instead of 200,000.
findings.evidence: In the PDF notes below the continued table: "Effective July 1, 2007, $200,000 of the net proceeds..." and in the 2007-2008 table row "Department of Commerce transfer +" the amount shown is 200,000. The HTML row shows "Department of Commerce transfer +" with 2007-2008 value "800,000" and 0.31%.

findings.description: A table value is incorrect for fiscal year 2008-2009: the Department of Commerce transfer amount was transcribed as 875,000 instead of 225,000.
findings.evidence: The PDF continued table shows in row "Department of Commerce transfer +" the 2008-2009 amount as 225,000. The HTML instead lists "875,000" for 2008-2009.

findings.description: A table value is incorrect for fiscal year 2009-2010: Local distribution allocations amount was transcribed as 10,860,329 instead of 10,869,329.
findings.evidence: In the PDF continued table, row "Local distribution allocations" under 2009-2010 reads "10,869,329" with "3.70%". The HTML shows "10,860,329" with "3.70%".

findings.description: The explanatory note for the Department of Commerce transfer is altered, changing the legal amount stated for 2008 from $25,000 to $75,000.
findings.evidence: PDF note states: "Effective October 1, 2008, SL 2008-107 amended the quarterly transfer provision stipulating that the original $200,000 allocation be designated for industry promotion and that an additional $25,000 of the net proceeds of tax collected on unfortified wine be designated on a quarterly basis for the purpose of research and development." The HTML changes this to "an additional $75,000..."

findings.description: Heading hierarchy is imperfect: the statutory subtitle is encoded as an h2 even though it is part of the table title block rather than a true subsection heading, and there are duplicate main containers.
findings.evidence:
HTML uses "

TABLE 12. NET ALCOHOLIC BEVERAGE TAX COLLECTIONS BY TYPE

" followed by "

[§ 105 ARTICLE 2C.]

" and later another "

TABLE 12. - Continued

". In the PDF these are title lines for the same table rather than section hierarchy.
